extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon won with a single late attack after CICCONE animated the climb, while FROOME Chris collapsed and lost over a minute.
tactical_patterns: CICCONE attacked repeatedly from breakaway despite being young domestique, softening favorites before YATES Simon's winning move.
BALLERINI Davide beat BELKOV Maxim in intermediate sprint despite latter's superior strength, revealing tactical confidence over raw power.
FROOME Chris required radio support mid-climb before collapse, showing GC distress manifests as communication dependency before time loss.
YATES Simon attacked only once at finish after sitting wheel entire climb, proving first acceleration matters more than early aggression.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: won stage by being patient and attacking once at finish after riding wheels, showing superior race economy
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: used radio mid-climb then lost over minute, revealing both physical and tactical distress before collapse
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: attacked repeatedly from breakaway despite youth, animated race before stage win, signals future aggressive capability
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: positive: outsprinted stronger BELKOV Maxim at intermediate by tactical positioning, shows racecraft beyond second-year experience
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: positive: only rider seated in final quartet with YATES PINOT POZZOVIVO, demonstrating superior climbing efficiency
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: dropped two bike lengths in finale while defending young rider jersey, marginal but controlled loss
